task_completion.description: T1 and T1ce MRI sequences are loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data module, Subject Hierarchy, or volume selector dropdowns show files or nodes corresponding to 'T1' and 'T1ce'.

task_completion.description: T2 and FLAIR MRI sequences are loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data module, Subject Hierarchy, or volume selector dropdowns show files or nodes corresponding to 'T2' and 'FLAIR'.

task_completion.description: The application layout is set to a multi-panel view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewing area is divided into multiple slice viewers (e.g., a 2x2 grid, 3D+3 slice, or 4-up layout) rather than a single large viewer.

task_completion.description: Each viewer panel displays a unique MRI sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible slice viewers show different image contrasts (e.g., one shows T1, another T1ce, another T2, another FLAIR), indicating different volumes are selected for each panel.

task_completion.description: The viewers are focused on the tumor region.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crosshairs or the displayed slices in the viewers intersect an area of abnormal brain tissue (the tumor), which typically appears as a hyperintense or hypointense lesion depending on the sequence.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the multi-panel layout is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screen Capture module is open with the output path set to '~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/mri_comparison.png', a save dialog is visible with this path, or a terminal/file manager shows the file successfully created at this location.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the application GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the 3D Slicer interface being actively used (e.g., clicking layout buttons, adjusting slice sliders, using the Screen Capture module), rather than just a terminal executing a script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The screenshot was generated from the actual loaded data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The captured image or the screen capture process should clearly originate from the 3D Slicer viewers, not from opening a pre-existing image file in a basic image viewer.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply rename an unrelated file to the target screenshot name.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshot file must visually match the multi-panel MRI layout configured in the application during the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The BraTS 2021 dataset contains exactly 4 MRI sequences per patient case: T1, T1ce, T2, and FLAIR. Each volume is standardized to dimensions of 240x240x155 voxels. The VLM should verify that all 4 distinct sequences are loaded and displayed simultaneously in the multi-panel layout.
